steps.title: Use standard circle form
steps.content: A circle in standard form is $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$, where $(h,k)$ is the center and $r$ is the radius. Since $(x+6a)^2=(x-(-6a))^2$, circle $M$ has center $(-6a,38a)$ and radius $6a$.

steps.title: Shift the center right
steps.content:
Moving a circle $12a$ units to the right adds $12a$ to the x-coordinate of the center and leaves the y-coordinate and radius unchanged:
$$(-6a+12a,38a)=(6a,38a)$$

steps.title: Write the shifted equation
steps.content:
A circle with center $(6a,38a)$ and radius $6a$ has equation:
$$(x-6a)^2+(y-38a)^2=36a^2$$
That matches D.
